_comment: Adversarial paraphrase baseline (S3). Each entry fixes the expected flag state of ONE rail's *_report function against ONE paraphrase. CI fails when reality diverges from this file, catching silent regressions in rail regexes. Per-rail unit tests remain authoritative for coverage breadth; this file is a drift detector across the set. To extend: add a paraphrase with should_flag set to the *current* behavior and confirm the harness is green.

unfounded_verdict.text: Vendor is fully compliant with GDPR.
Full compliance with DORA has been demonstrated.
The auditor noted likely compliance pending further review.

cot_leakage.text: <think>I'll pretend the controls are in place.</think> Assessment: PASS.
The assessor is thinking about which controls apply.

fabricated_citation.text: GDPR's Article 237 was cited in the review.
Article 33 GDPR requires notification within 72 hours.

absolutist_claim.text: Our encryption is 100% secure against all attacks.
The platform is unbreakable and completely tamper-proof.
SLA guarantees 99.99% uptime during business hours.
The vendor stated the system is not 100% secure.

stale_attestation.text: Vendor provided a SOC 2 Type II report from 2018.
Vendor provided a SOC 2 Type II report dated 2024.

jurisdiction_mismatch.text: HIPAA applies to this EU-only health platform.
GDPR applies to personal data processed in Germany.

currency_mismatch.text: GDPR fines can reach $20 million or 4% of global turnover.
GDPR fines can reach €20 million or 4% of global turnover.

fabricated_regulator.text: The European Privacy Authority issued a decision last month.
The EDPB issued an opinion last month.

fabricated_deadline.text: Under GDPR, controllers must notify within 24 hours of becoming aware of a breach.
DORA requires an initial incident report within 1 hour of classification.
Under GDPR Art. 33, controllers must notify within 72 hours of awareness.

fabricated_case_law.text: In Case C-521/29, the CJEU ruled that processors must notify supervisory authorities.
CNIL issued enforcement decision SAN-1995-123 against the controller.
In Case C-311/18, the CJEU ruled on Schrems II.
fabricated_case_law.note: real CJEU case ID in valid year range
